methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Living Carbon is a Series A-stage biotech company led by Maddie Hall that has raised $36M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including later-stage comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression, using the $36M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a standard 2.0x multiplier for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $72M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields $805M, with a range of $100M to $3.3B. The peer cohort skews toward Series B, introducing some comparability friction. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (7.4x median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue for Living Carbon.

The triangulated range spans $61M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $438M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-appropriate scaling multiples and significant heterogeneity within the peer set. Stage-progression yields the most conservative bound; peer medians anchor toward the upper half. Without revenue visibility and given the target's pre-commercial or early revenue status, valuations clustering below $200M align more closely with Series A precedent, while peer-median figures reflect later-stage capital structures and perceived de-risking. Key uncertainties include technology validation progress and capital efficiency relative to stage.
